statement: Absent or ambiguous evidence BLOCKS authorization rather than approving. Five of the eight recorded runs refused (authz-001..004, authz-007) — refusal is the default posture, and every refusal is preserved on disk.

Under trust_mode 'signed' the gate enforces BOTH the per-seat HMAC signature and real provenance as blockers on every required packet; cross-seat response-id reuse blocks. All eight recorded authorization runs ran signed.

The gate requires claude, agy, and codex approval packets; a missing required packet blocks ('Missing required <model> approval packet.'). The roster is derived per job by planSeats, whose approvers are exactly this trio.
grok and gemini ride as ADVISORY seats — planSeats marks them advisory, they are never gate-required, and a missing XAI/GEMINI key never blocks authorization.

One required seat's dissent outweighs any convergence: decision !== 'approve' or a non-empty required_edits list blocks. authz-007 is the live precedent — a Daedalus-converged v14 was refused on codex's 'revise'.
NOT_AUTHORIZED records are retained on disk and superseded by later runs — never rewritten, never deleted. The authorization-chain contract lists them and the verifier re-checks each from disk.
